title: jose SignJWT.setProtectedHeader with alg: "none" — the JWT is issued unsigned and forgeable

params.passDetail: The protected header pins a real signing algorithm (not "none") — the issued JWT carries a verifiable signature.
params.failDetail: setProtectedHeader is called with alg: "none", so jose issues an UNSECURED JWT with no signature. Anyone can craft a token with alg: none and arbitrary claims; wherever "none" is accepted it authenticates — a complete auth bypass. Set alg to a real algorithm (e.g. "HS256", "RS256", "EdDSA").
params.absentCallDetail: No setProtectedHeader call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: setProtectedHeader does not set alg to a literal; verify the resolved algorithm is not "none".

provenance.note: jose is the modern JWT library; alg: "none" in setProtectedHeader is the same unsigned-token footgun as jsonwebtoken's, reached through a different API. Copied from a snippet that 'skips signing to test claims', it ships an auth system anyone can forge against.
